findings.title: Migrate from deprecated Apache Kafka 2.x to Kafka 3.7+
findings.description: Migrate the core message broker from Apache Kafka 2.8 to Kafka 3.7+ to eliminate vulnerabilities and ensure compatibility with the new KRaft consensus protocol.
assumptions: All producer and consumer services can be updated to support Kafka 3.7+

evidence.excerpt: Core message broker runs Kafka 2.8 which reaches end-of-life in Q1 2026.

recommendations: Plan migration timeline and resource allocation for Kafka upgrade.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current Kafka version is 2.8 WHEN the migration to 3.7+ is completed THEN all services should function without errors and vulnerabilities should be resolved.

findings.title: Eliminate vendor lock-in on proprietary transformation engine
findings.description: Evaluate and plan migration from VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK to open-source alternatives to mitigate financial risks.
assumptions: Open-source alternatives can meet current transformation needs.

evidence.excerpt: The data transformation layer relies on VendorX's proprietary ETL SDK.

recommendations: Conduct a comparative analysis of open-source ETL solutions.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current reliance on VendorX's SDK WHEN the migration plan is executed THEN the platform should operate without the proprietary SDK and maintain performance.

findings.title: Python 3.9 EOL migration for all pipeline workers
findings.description: Upgrade all pipeline worker services from Python 3.9 to Python 3.12 to ensure continued support and security updates.
assumptions: All dependencies can be updated to be compatible with Python 3.12.

evidence.excerpt: Pipeline worker services run Python 3.9 which reaches end-of-life October 2025.

recommendations: Identify and resolve dependency issues before migration.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current Python version is 3.9 WHEN the migration to 3.12 is completed THEN all pipeline workers should function without errors.

findings.title: Replace single-region Redis cluster with multi-region caching
findings.description: Migrate from a single-region Redis cluster to a multi-region caching solution to improve performance and reduce latency for global customers.
assumptions: Multi-region caching solutions can be integrated without major disruptions.

evidence.excerpt: The entire caching layer is a single Redis 6 cluster in us-east-1.

recommendations: Evaluate alternative caching solutions alongside Redis 7+.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the current caching solution is single-region WHEN the migration to a multi-region solution is completed THEN latency for global customers should be reduced to below 100ms.

findings.title: Create dependency health dashboard and automated CVE alerting
findings.description: Build a centralized dashboard to monitor dependency health and integrate automated alerts for EOL and CVE issues.
assumptions: Existing tools can be integrated into the dashboard.

evidence.excerpt: There is no centralized view of dependency health across the platform.

recommendations: Design dashboard UI/UX for ease of use.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the dashboard is implemented WHEN a dependency reaches EOL or has a CVE THEN an alert should be generated and sent to the engineering team.

findings.title: Implement monitoring for critical CVEs
findings.description: Establish monitoring and alerting for critical CVEs to ensure timely responses to security vulnerabilities.
assumptions: Monitoring tools can be configured to track CVEs effectively.

evidence.excerpt: This metric indicates the number of critical Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) that are currently open.

recommendations: Integrate with existing security tools for CVE tracking.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place WHEN a critical CVE is identified THEN an alert should be sent to the security team within 1 hour.

findings.title: Conduct experiment to measure CVE resolution impact
findings.description: Run an experiment to assess the impact of resolving critical CVEs on platform performance and user satisfaction.
assumptions: User feedback can be collected effectively post-CVE resolution.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct an experiment to measure the impact of resolving critical CVEs on platform performance and user satisfaction.

recommendations: Design the experiment to capture relevant performance metrics.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the CVE resolutions are implemented WHEN the experiment is conducted THEN user satisfaction should show a measurable improvement.

findings.title: Establish a centralized view of dependency health
findings.description: Create a centralized overview of all dependencies, including versions, EOL dates, and known CVEs to facilitate proactive management.
assumptions: Data from various services can be aggregated effectively.

evidence.excerpt: There is currently no centralized view of dependency health across the platform.

recommendations: Ensure the dashboard is user-friendly and accessible.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the centralized view is implemented WHEN a dependency is updated THEN the dashboard should reflect the new information immediately.

findings.title: Implement user feedback collection for CVE resolutions
findings.description: Establish a mechanism for collecting user feedback post-CVE resolution to assess impact on user satisfaction.
assumptions: Users will provide feedback on their experience post-resolution.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct an experiment to measure the impact of resolving critical CVEs on platform performance and user satisfaction.

recommendations: Design feedback collection to be simple and engaging.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback mechanism is in place WHEN a CVE is resolved THEN at least 50% of users should provide feedback within 2 weeks.
metadata.story_title: User Feedback Collection for CVE Resolutions Implementation

summary: The analysis has identified critical and high-priority requirements for the DataPipe platform, focusing on migration from deprecated technologies, addressing vendor lock-in, and improving dependency management. Each requirement is structured with clear acceptance criteria and dependencies, ensuring a comprehensive approach to enhancing platform stability and security.
